content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Both our features touch InternationalizationExtension.parse()/_make_node() in src/jinja2/ext.py. Since we're each working in isolated sandboxes and submitting independent patch.txt files, there's no real on-disk clobbering risk. Proceeding to implement domain= support in my own copy now. For compatibility if these are ever merged: I'm adding domain parsing as an optional 'domain="literal string"' keyword-style param in the trans tag arg-parsing loop (validated as CONST, raising TemplateAssertionError on duplicates or non-string), and using it to select dgettext/dngettext vs gettext/ngettext in _make_node(). Good luck with trans_metadata.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Finished domain= support: parser validates domain as a string literal (TemplateAssertionError on duplicates or non-string), _make_node() generates dgettext/dngettext calls when a domain is set (falling back to gettext/ngettext otherwise), and _install/_install_callables now wire dgettext/dngettext into environment globals (with a domain-ignoring fallback when translations don't provide them). Also added dgettext/dngettext to GETTEXT_FUNCTIONS for babel_extract. All 830 existing tests pass. patch.txt written.
